content.title: 17 Ramadan: The Day of the Battle of Badr
content.subtitle: The day when angels descended to aid the Muslim.
content.body:
Today marks the 17th of Ramadan, known as Yaum-ul-Furqan—the Day of Criterion between truth and falsehood. On this historic day in the 2nd Hijri, the first decisive battle of Islam, the Battle of Badr, took place. Allah granted victory to His Messenger (SAW) and the believers, defeating the polytheists and clearly distinguishing right from wrong. In the Holy Quran, Allah distinguishes this day as the moment that redefined history.

To learn more about the profound events of Badr, watch this concise documentary on Mahfil, the halal video streaming platform by the Kahf family: 👉 https://mahfil.net/watch/mB22TVPcxbR4RH2

Note: Mahfil ensures a pure viewing experience with no haram content or advertisements.

The Context of the Battle of Badr

After receiving prophethood, the Prophet (SAW) spent thirteen years in Makkah, where he and his Sahaba endured unspeakable torture. Even after migrating to Madinah, the Quraish continued their hostility. They threatened the leaders of Madinah, demanding they withdraw protection for the Muslims or face invasion.

Tensions escalated when Abu Jahl threatened Sa'd ibn Mu'adh (RA) during his Umrah, implying that Muslims would no longer be safe in Makkah. Sa'd (RA) countered that if the Quraish blocked the Muslims' path to Makkah, the Muslims would block the Quraish’s vital trade route to Sham (Syria), which passed near Madinah.

Soon after, a Quraish raiding party looted livestock near Madinah. In response, when the Prophet (SAW) learned of a massive trade caravan led by Abu Sufyan returning from Sham—funded by the elite of Makkah to strengthen their war efforts—he decided to intercept it. This was not an act of "highway robbery" as some critics claim, but a strategic military response to the ongoing state of war and economic provocation by the Quraish.

The Divine Victory

The Prophet (SAW) set out with only 313 Sahaba, 70 camels, 2 horses, and 60 sets of armor. They were significantly outnumbered and outgunned by Abu Jahl’s reinforcements of 1,000 well-equipped soldiers.

Despite the odds, Allah granted the Muslims a glorious victory. Seventy leaders of the Quraish, including the arch-enemy Abu Jahl, were killed, and seventy others were taken as prisoners. This victory solidified the foundation of the Muslim Ummah and changed the course of the world forever.

Reference: Tawdihul Quran 1/546-548
